rulings.Case Title: YING Q. SUNG VS. GARY NELSON ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 7, 2021 line 2. DEFENDANTS' NOTICE OF DEMURRER AND DEMURRER TO THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT AND MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS THEREOF Demurrer to causes of action 1 to 4 is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff shall have final leave to amend to allege facts in support of each of these causes of action (Plaintiff must allege facts, not conclusions or contentions, e.g. allegations in paragraph 35 are conclusions not supported by facts as to failure to exercise due diligence in conducting repairs once undertaken, failure to follow appropriate industry repair, etc.). Plaintiff shall also allege damages caused by the specific wrongful acts giving rise to each cause of action. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). rulings.Case Title: MAURICIO MAGANA CASTILLO VS. AARON GRUVER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Special Motion And Special Motion To Strike Cross- Claims One And Three
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 7, 2021 line 3. PLAINTIFF MAURICIO MAGANA CASTILLO'S NOTICE OF SPECIAL MOTION AND SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS-CLAIMS ONE AND THREE is GRANTED. Moving party satisfied its burden under the first prong of the anti-slapp. Cross-Complaint asserts claims for Slander of Title and Abuse of Process arising out of protected activity. ("Cross-Defendant filed the Complaint on June 9, 2021, thereafter recorded notices of pendency of action upon Unit 202 and Unit 303, claiming some interest in each of the Subject Properties, and casting doubt upon Cross-Complainant's ownership."). Opposing party failed to show likelihood of prevailing on the merits re: privileged communications. CC 47(b)(4). Lis pendens identified an action "previously filed" in a court of competent jurisdiction that affects title or right to possession of real property, i.e. a claim in a pleading which would, if meritorious, affect title to real property. Alpha & Omega Dev., LP v. Whillock Contracting, Inc. (2012) 200 Cal.App.4th 656. The evidentiary support or merits of such claim are irrelevant to this analysis. Additionally, recordation of lis pendens and filing/maintenance of a lawsuit cannot support a cause of action for abuse of process. Woodcourt II Limited v. McDonald Co. (1981) 119 Cal.App.3d 245; JSJ Limited Partnership v. Mehrban (2012) 205 Cal.App.4th 1512. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCO EAGLE BAR LLC, FORMERLY KNOWN AS VS. VOULA NIKITOPOULOS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 7, 2021 line 4. DEFENDANTS' NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO EXPUNGE LIS PENDENS Hearing Required. Assuming for the purposes of this question that (1) the buyer's name is disclosed on December 7, 2021, and (2) Plaintiffs are provided 7 days to exercise their right of first refusal, are Plaintiffs able, ready and willing to proceed with the purchase procedures on the terms of the buyer's offer as originally presented and re-affirmed in buyer's declaration? Plaintiffs to file a declaration stating the answer to this question limited to "yes" or "no" no later than December 7, 2021 by 9:00 a.m. Defendant to file a declaration no later than 9:00 a.m. on December 7, 2021 stating whether buyer will agree to the disclosure of their name (subject to a protective order). Declaration shall be limited to "yes" or "no." Assuming that the answer is "yes", and if the name is disclosed on December 7 , 2021 (subject to a protective order), hearing will be continued to December 17, 2021 at which time Plaintiff shall state whether the right has been exercised within seven days, and to be prepared to (a) execute a PSA (to be prepared by Defendant), (b) deposit $50,000 into escrow (to be opened by Plaintiff by December 17, 2021) and (c) show its ability to close per the terms of the agreement (financial statements, loan approval letters, etc.) - evidence to be submitted no later than December 15, 2021 by noon. If Plaintiffs response to the question (are Plaintiffs able, ready and willing to proceed with the purchase procedures on the terms of the buyer's offer as originally presented and re-affirmed in buyer's declaration) is "no" the Court will render its decision after the hearing. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: BPREP ONE POST, LLC VS. TOTIENT CAPITAL, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 7, 2021 line 5. DEFENDANT BRAVADO NETWORK, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled. Breach of Consent agreement is sufficiently stated and is not uncertain.
